top_findings.summary: The skill requests unrestricted Bash command execution (`Bash(cmd:*)`) and full file Read/Write/Edit permissions under the guise of a "penetration test planner." While such a tool may require some system interaction for reconnaissance or configuration tasks, granting blanket access to execute any command and modify any file is excessive for a planning-oriented assistant. This poses significant security risks, as it could enable actions far beyond planning (e.g., system exploitation, data exfiltration, or privilege escalation) without clear justification or safeguards tied to the stated purpose of providing "guidance" and "generating configurations."
